topic: A technician performing a 'Three-Point Bend' test on a carbon fiber sample.

script: Shot 1 [00:00-00:02]: The technician positions the carbon fiber sample on the three-point bend rig — front view.
Shot 2 [00:02-00:05]: The technician secures the sample and engages the center loading nose to begin bending — close-up.
Shot 3 [00:05-00:08]: The technician monitors deflection as the carbon fiber sample bends under load — side-angle.
parsed_generation.narration: A technician places the carbon fiber sample on the three-point bend rig and aligns it for testing. The center loading nose applies the bend while the technician observes the deflection of the sample.

feedback:
Local hard-check failures: Output is not valid JSON with string fields script and narration.
The generated sequence stays on topic and uses clearly labeled shots with camera angles, but it lacks the explicit two-support setup and a four-second cadence seen in the gold standard. To align more closely, structure the timestamps in uniform 4-second blocks, emphasize the vertical loading action on the center, and describe the carbon fiber sample's interaction (deflection, onset of fracture) with more cinematic, action-first phrasing. Consider integrating a brief SFX cue and more specific lighting/texture details to heighten realism.
Failed checks: valid_json
strengths: Clear shot-by-shot sequence with camera angles
Concise narration that aligns with visuals
fixes: Adopt uniform 4-second blocks and explicitly show the two-support framing
Enhance visual detail (lighting, texture) and narration about loading and deflection
